title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for game state, scenes, pause, life loss, restart, and ownership and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python State Model · changed case · Quiz State Model
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9471 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to model menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, and restart transitions without hidden scene state; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test every legal and illegal transition including focus loss, quit, device loss, corrupted settings, and rapid restart.
